title: The Personal Trainer
introKo:
새로 등록한 헬스장에서 받는 아주 건전한 첫 트레이닝 수업.
…트레이너의 자세 교정이 유난히 밀착형이라는 것만 빼면요.
introEn:
Your first personal training session. Very hands-on.
...strictly professional, of course.

quiz.q: 첫 수업에서 트레이너 Alex가 제일 먼저 시킨 것은 뭐였나요?

normal.text: Monday evening. Your first personal training lesson at the new gym. Your trainer, Alex, is waiting for you with a warm smile.
"Welcome," he says. "Tonight you are all mine. One hour, just the two of us. Are you ready to sweat?"
"First, we stretch. A cold body is a stiff body. Don't worry — I will be very hands-on."

options.reaction: "It means I fix your form with my hands," he says. "You will like the results."
"Anything?" He smiles slowly. "I like that spirit."

normal.text: You bend forward to stretch. "Further," Alex says, right beside your ear. "Breathe. Let your body open."
"You are very tight," he says. "That is okay. Tight is normal for a first time. We will loosen you up slowly."

options.reaction: "Of course. Trust me — I have done this position a hundred times."

options.en: Don't worry — I'm more flexible than I look.
options.reaction: "Is that so?" he laughs. "Then let's find out. Flexibility shows in practice — lots of practice."

normal.text: Next, squats. In the mirror, Alex is standing very close behind you. "Lower," he says. "Slower. Feel every second of it."
Your heart is beating fast. Alex glances at your watch and smiles. "Your heart rate is up. That means your body likes this."
"One more set," he says. You groan, but he only smiles. "Trust me. The best part always comes at the end."

options.en: Okay. But my legs are already jelly.
options.ko: 알았어요. 그런데 저 벌써 다리가 후들거려요.
options.reaction: "Jelly is progress," he says. "One more set turns jelly into steel."

options.en: Fine. But you owe me a break.
options.ko: 좋아요. 대신 나중에 쉬게 해 줘요.
options.reaction: "Deal. Finish strong, and I will take good care of you after."

normal.text: You finish the last set. Your legs are shaking, your shirt is wet, and you can't stop smiling.
"Do not run away yet," Alex says, pointing to a mat in the corner. "A good lesson always ends with a cool-down. Lie down. This part is my job — you just breathe."

options.reaction: "Safe," Alex repeats slowly, like a new word. "Sure. Let's call it that."

options.en: So this is the best part you promised.
options.ko: 그러니까 이게 아까 약속한 '제일 좋은 부분'이군요.
options.reaction: "You remembered." His smile grows wider. "Good students always get a little extra."

normal.text: "Leg up," Alex says. "From here, your only job is breathing." The stretch goes slow and steady. "Tell me if it is too much." Somehow, you never say a word.
"Same time next week?" Alex asks, handing you a towel. "You will be sore tomorrow. That is how you know it worked."

hard.text: Monday evening finds you at the new gym for your very first personal training session. Your trainer, Alex, is already waiting, a towel over his shoulder and a warm smile on his face.
"Welcome," he says, looking you up and down like a project he cannot wait to start. "For the next hour you are all mine — just the two of us. Ready to work up a sweat?"
"We begin with stretching. A cold body is a stiff body, and I refuse to work with a stiff body. Fair warning — my style is very hands-on."

options.en: Hands-on? What exactly does that mean?
options.reaction: "It means I correct your form with my hands," he explains smoothly. "You will see why everyone comes back."
"Anything?" He smiles slowly. "I will remember that when we get to the last set."

hard.text: You bend forward to stretch. "Further," Alex says, his voice suddenly right beside your ear. "Breathe. Let your body open up."
"You are extremely tight," he observes. "Which is perfectly normal for a first time. We will loosen you up — slowly, properly, no rushing."

options.reaction: "Absolutely. Trust me — I have done this position more times than I can count."

options.en: Don't worry — I'm more flexible than I look.
options.reaction: "Is that so?" he laughs. "Then let's find out. Flexibility shows in practice — a great deal of practice."

hard.text: Next come the squats. In the mirror, you can see Alex standing very close behind you. "Lower," he says. "Slower. I want you to feel every single second of it."
Your heart is pounding by now. Alex glances at your watch with a satisfied smile. "Your heart rate is climbing fast. That is exactly what I want to see."
"One more set," he announces. You groan, but he only leans in closer. "Trust me on this one. The best part always comes right at the end."

options.en: Okay. But my legs are already jelly.
options.ko: 알았어요. 그런데 저 벌써 다리가 후들거려요.
options.reaction: "Jelly is progress," he says. "One more set is exactly how jelly turns into steel."

options.en: Fine. But afterwards you owe me a break.
options.ko: 좋아요. 대신 나중에 쉬게 해 줘요.
options.reaction: "Deal. Finish strong, and afterwards I promise to take very good care of you."

hard.text: You push through the final set. Your legs are shaking, your shirt is soaked through, and somehow you cannot stop smiling.
"Not so fast," Alex says, nodding toward a mat in the corner. "A proper lesson always ends with a cool-down. Lie down and relax — this part of the evening is entirely my job."

options.en: Okay. A cool-down sounds safe enough.
options.reaction: "Safe," Alex repeats, tasting the word. "Of course. Let's call it that for now."

options.en: So this is the best part you promised me.
options.ko: 그러니까 이게 아까 나한테 약속한 '제일 좋은 부분'이군요.
options.reaction: "You remembered." His smile grows a shade wider. "Attentive students always earn a little extra time."

hard.text: "Leg up," Alex says. "From here, your only job is breathing." The stretch goes slow and patient. "Tell me the moment it becomes too much." Strangely, that moment never comes.
"Same time next week?" Alex asks, handing you a towel with a knowing look. "You will be wonderfully sore tomorrow. That is how you know it worked."

admit.en: to say that something is true, even when it is hard to say

announces.en: says something in a strong, clear way

answer.en: a reply to a question or message

beating.en: moving in a steady rhythm, like a heart

bend.en: to lean your body forward or down

beside.en: right next to someone or something

book.en: to reserve a time or place

brave.en: not afraid; ready to face fear

breathe.en: to take air in and out

breathing.en: the act of taking air in and out

brought.en: carried or caused to come (past of bring)

call.en: to name something; to say it is

care.ko: 돌봄 (take care of: 잘 챙겨 주다)

checked.en: looked to make sure (past of check)

clearly.en: in a way easy to see or understand

complained.en: said you were unhappy about something

confidence.en: the feeling that you can do it

cooldown.en: slow, easy exercise after a workout

corner.en: the place where two walls meet

count.en: to say numbers one by one

deal.ko: 약속, 거래 (Deal: 좋아요, 콜) / a great deal of: 아주 많은
deal.en: an agreement; okay, let's do it

do.en: to make or perform an action

does.en: form of do for he or she

ear.en: the body part you hear with

earn.en: to get something by working for it

endings.en: the last parts; how things finish

even.en: used to show something is surprising

exactly.en: in a precise way; just right

exception.en: something the rule does not include

fair.en: honest; fair warning means early warning

feel.en: to sense something in your body

form.en: the way your body moves in exercise

found.en: discovered; thought it was (past of find)

get.en: to reach a place or point

grins.en: smiles in a big, playful way

groan.en: to make a low, tired sound

guessing.ko: 추측하다 (guess의 진행형; keep guessing: 계속 헷갈리게 하다)
guessing.en: trying to know without being sure

guide.en: to lead or show the way

handson.en: using your hands directly to teach

hips.en: the wide part below your waist

jelly.en: a soft, shaky food — how very tired legs feel

kind.ko: 종류 (that kind of talk: 그런 말)

know.en: to have information in your mind

leg.en: the body part you walk with

legs.en: the parts of your body for walking

lie.en: to put your body flat, as on a bed

lower.en: closer to the ground; further down

make.en: to create; make an exception means allow

mat.en: a soft flat pad for the floor

match.ko: 맞수, 임자 (meet one's match: 임자를 만나다)
match.en: a person as strong as you

mercy.en: kindness to someone in your power

met.en: came face to face (past of meet)

mirror.en: glass that shows what is in front of it

monday.en: the first day of the work week

negotiate.en: to talk to reach an agreement

negotiator.en: a person who makes deals well

nodding.en: moving your head to point or agree

notebook.en: a small book for writing notes

noticed.en: saw and paid attention (past of notice)

nowhere.en: no place; out of nowhere means suddenly

opened.en: started or made open (past of open)

owe.en: to have to give something back

person.en: a human; in person means face to face

plan.en: an idea of what to do

played.en: acted; played it safe means took no risks

position.en: the way your body is placed

practice.en: doing something many times to improve

promise.en: to say you will surely do something; words you must keep

promised.en: said you would surely do it (past of promise)

promises.en: says something will surely happen; things you promise

promising.en: saying you will surely do something

proud.en: feeling good about what you did

push.en: to keep going even when hard

read.en: to look at words and understand

relax.en: to rest and let your body go loose

responsible.en: careful and doing what is right

rest.ko: 휴식; 나머지 (the rest of: ~의 나머지); 얹히다
rest.en: a short break; the part that is left; to sit or lie on something

results.en: what happens after work or effort

road.en: a way to go; a path

row.en: one after another; in a line

schedule.en: a plan of times for things

second.en: a very short moment of time

send.en: to make a message go to someone

sentence.en: a group of words with a full meaning

sequel.en: the next story after this one

seriously.en: in a real, not joking way

shaking.en: moving quickly from side to side

shoulder.en: the body part between neck and arm

shower.en: washing your body under falling water

side.en: one part of a person or thing

sign.en: a board with words on it

smiled.en: made a happy face (past of smile)

something.en: a thing you do not name

squats.en: exercises where you bend your knees

starters.ko: 시작하는 사람들 (slow starter: 시동이 느린 사람)
starters.en: people who begin; slow starters begin slowly

stretch.en: to make your body long and loose

style.en: a special way of doing things

sweat.en: water your skin makes when working hard

tasting.en: trying the feel of something, like food

titled.en: with the name or title of

towel.en: a cloth for drying your body

trust.en: to believe someone is good and honest

underlined.en: with a line drawn under it

us.ko: 우리를 (the two of us: 우리 둘)

voice.en: the sound a person makes when speaking

warning.en: words that tell of coming danger

watch.en: a small clock on your wrist

weeks.en: of the week; more than one week

welcome.en: a friendly hello to a guest

which.en: a word that points back to something

work.ko: 운동하다, 일하다 (work up a sweat: 땀을 빼다)
work.en: to exercise hard; to do a job

wink.handson: '직접 손으로 지도한다'는 훌륭한 교육 방식이에요. 알렉스는 이 방식에 유난히 진심일 뿐이고요.
wink.stretch: 몸을 풀어 주는 준비 운동이에요. 무엇을 준비하는지는 각자의 상상에 맡길게요.
wink.tight: 근육이 뭉쳤다는 말이에요. 처음이라 그런 거라고… 트레이너는 말하죠.
wink.position: 운동 자세를 뜻해요. 다만 '이 자세는 수도 없이 해 봤다'는 트레이너의 말은 어쩐지 묘하게 들려요.
wink.flexibility: 유연성. 운동에 꼭 필요하죠. 다른 데에도 쓸모가 많다고들 해요.
wink.sweat: 땀은 건강한 운동의 증거예요. 물론 땀 흘리는 방법이 운동만 있는 건 아니지만요.
wink.set: '한 세트만 더'는 헬스장에서 늘 듣는 말이지만, 다른 상황에서도 종종 들리는 말이에요.
wink.sore: 다음 날 찾아오는 뻐근한 근육통이에요. 물론 몸이 뻐근해지는 이유가 운동만 있는 건 아니라고들 하죠.
wink.comes: '오다'라는 평범한 동사예요. '제일 좋은 건 마지막에 온다'는 운동의 오랜 격언이죠. 운동만의 격언은 아니라고들 하지만요.
wink.cooldown: 운동을 마무리하는 정리 단계예요. 하필 트레이너가 '오늘 밤 제일 좋은 부분'이라고 부르는 단계이기도 하죠.

winkEn.handson: A perfectly respectable teaching method — using your hands. Alex just takes it unusually seriously.
winkEn.stretch: A warm-up that prepares the body. What exactly it is preparing for is left to your imagination.
winkEn.tight: It just means stiff muscles. It is your first time, after all — or so the trainer says.
winkEn.position: A simple exercise stance. Though a trainer saying he has 'done this position a hundred times' lands a little differently.
winkEn.flexibility: Essential for exercise, of course. People say it comes in handy elsewhere, too.
winkEn.sweat: Proof of an honest workout. Though exercise is hardly the only way to work one up.
winkEn.set: 'One more set' is classic gym talk. It has been heard in other rooms as well.
winkEn.sore: That next-day muscle ache. Though, as they say, workouts are not the only thing that leaves you feeling it.
winkEn.comes: A perfectly innocent verb. 'The best part comes at the end' is old gym wisdom — and, some say, not only gym wisdom.
winkEn.cooldown: The gentle wind-down at the end of a workout. It also happens to be the part Alex calls the best of the night.

0000.name: 신중한 교과서 회원
0000.en: You asked every question, checked every position, and chose the safe road to the very end. Alex writes one line in his notebook: 'Perfect student. Almost too perfect.' Next Monday, he promises, the questions will be his.
0000.ko: 질문은 전부 하고, 자세는 전부 확인하고, 끝까지 안전한 길만 골랐어요. 알렉스는 수첩에 한 줄을 적어요. '완벽한 수강생. 완벽해도 너무 완벽함.' 다음 주 월요일엔 질문을 자기가 하겠다고 하네요.

0001.name: 마지막에 눈뜬 수강생
0001.en: You were careful all lesson — until the cool-down, when you suddenly remembered his promise about the best part. Alex noticed. Your next session is booked for ninety minutes, 'because endings deserve time.'
0001.ko: 수업 내내 조심조심하다가, 쿨다운에서 갑자기 '제일 좋은 부분' 약속을 기억해 냈죠. 알렉스는 그 순간을 놓치지 않았어요. 다음 세션은 90분으로 예약됐답니다 — '마무리엔 시간이 필요하니까'라면서요.

0010.name: 조용한 협상가
0010.en: You played it safe all night — except once, when you calmly made a deal for a break. Alex respects a good negotiator. Your next program arrives with a new line at the bottom: 'Rest times to be discussed in person.'
0010.ko: 밤새 안전하게만 가다가 딱 한 번, 아주 침착하게 휴식 협상을 했어요. 알렉스는 협상 잘하는 사람을 존중하죠. 다음 프로그램 맨 아래엔 새 문장이 하나 생겼어요. '휴식 시간은 만나서 직접 상의할 것.'

0011.name: 뒷심 좋은 수강생
0011.en: You started shy, but by the end you were making deals and asking for the best part he promised. Alex likes a strong finish. He walks you to the door and says the next lesson will start where this one ended.
0011.ko: 시작은 수줍었지만, 끝에 가서는 협상도 하고 '제일 좋은 부분'까지 챙겼죠. 알렉스는 뒷심 좋은 사람을 좋아해요. 문까지 배웅하며 말하네요 — 다음 수업은 오늘 끝난 바로 그 지점에서 시작하자고요.

0100.name: 숨은 유연성 보유자
0100.en: So careful, so polite — and then, out of nowhere, 'more flexible than I look.' Alex wrote that sentence down, word for word. Your next program has a whole new stretching section, built just for you.
0100.ko: 그렇게 조심스럽고 예의 바르더니, 난데없이 '보기보다 유연하다'니요. 알렉스는 그 문장을 토씨 하나 안 틀리게 받아 적었어요. 다음 프로그램엔 당신만을 위한 스트레칭 섹션이 통째로 새로 생겼답니다.

0101.name: 은근한 반전 매력
0101.en: Polite questions, careful answers — and then flexibility talk, and then asking about the best part. Alex says quiet people surprise him the most. He says it while writing your name at the top of Monday's schedule.
0101.ko: 예의 바른 질문, 조심스러운 대답 — 그러다 유연성 이야기가 나오고, 마지막엔 '제일 좋은 부분'까지 물었죠. 알렉스는 조용한 사람이 제일 놀랍다고 해요. 그 말을 하면서 월요일 일정 맨 위에 당신 이름을 적고 있고요.

0110.name: 중반 각성형
0110.en: You began with careful questions, then somewhere in the stretching you stopped asking and started promising — flexibility, deals, all of it. The cool-down brought back your shy side. Alex finds the mix 'very educational.'
0110.ko: 조심스러운 질문으로 시작했는데, 스트레칭이 끝나 갈 때쯤 질문을 멈추고 약속을 하기 시작했죠 — 유연성이니 협상이니 전부요. 쿨다운에서는 다시 수줍은 모습으로 돌아왔지만요. 알렉스는 이 조합이 '아주 교육적'이라네요.

0111.name: 슬로 스타터
0111.en: One careful question at the start — then no more questions, only promises. Alex says slow starters always finish the strongest. He says it with the face of a man who has seen the data.
0111.ko: 시작할 때 조심스러운 질문 하나 — 그 뒤로는 질문 대신 약속만 남았죠. 알렉스는 슬로 스타터가 제일 강하게 마무리한다고 해요. 지금까지 수강생을 전부 지켜본 사람의 표정으로요.

1000.name: 첫마디만 과감한 타입
1000.en: 'Ready for anything,' you said — and then spent the rest of the night proving how carefully you can do anything. Alex calls it the safest kind of brave. He books you in for Monday anyway, smiling.
1000.ko: '뭐든 할 준비 됐다'더니, 그 뒤로는 밤새 얼마나 조심스럽게 뭐든 하는지 증명했어요. 알렉스는 그걸 '세상에서 제일 안전한 용감함'이라고 부르죠. 그래도 웃으면서 월요일 예약을 잡아 두네요.

1001.name: 처음과 끝이 강한 타입
1001.en: You opened with 'ready for anything' and closed by asking about the best part. The middle was very responsible. Alex says beginnings and endings are what people remember — and he clearly plans to be memorable.
1001.ko: '뭐든 준비됐다'로 열고, '제일 좋은 부분'으로 닫았죠. 중간은 아주 모범적이었고요. 알렉스는 사람들이 기억하는 건 처음과 끝이라고 해요 — 본인은 확실히 기억에 남을 계획인가 봐요.

1010.name: 밀당의 정석
1010.en: Bold, then careful, then bold again — you kept Alex guessing all night, and he loved every minute of it. 'You train like you negotiate,' he says at the door. It did not sound like a complaint.
1010.ko: 과감했다가, 조심했다가, 다시 과감했다가 — 밤새 알렉스를 헷갈리게 했고, 그는 그 모든 순간을 즐겼어요. '운동을 꼭 협상처럼 하시네요.' 문 앞에서 그가 말해요. 싫다는 말투는 아니었어요.

1011.name: 한 번 숨 고른 승부사
1011.en: You charged ahead all night and slowed down exactly once — to check if the position was normal. Alex found that one careful moment 'adorable.' He used that word. You have already booked next week.
1011.ko: 밤새 밀어붙이다가 딱 한 번 속도를 늦췄죠 — 자세가 정상인지 확인할 때요. 알렉스는 그 유일하게 조심스러운 순간이 '귀엽다'고 했어요. 진짜 그 단어를 썼다니까요. 다음 주 예약은 이미 끝났고요.

1100.name: 초반 전력 질주형
1100.en: Ready for anything, flexible as promised — the first half of your lesson was pure confidence. Then you turned careful, and Alex smiled like a man saving the best chapter for later. He wants a sequel.
1100.ko: 뭐든 준비됐고, 예고대로 유연했고 — 수업 전반전은 자신감 그 자체였어요. 그러다 후반에 조심스러워졌죠. 알렉스는 제일 좋은 챕터를 아껴 두는 사람처럼 웃었어요. 속편을 원한대요.

1101.name: 잠깐 쉬어 간 도전자
1101.en: Bold from the first word, you slowed down exactly once — to admit your legs had turned to jelly. Alex has not forgotten it. Next Monday's plan says 'special care' right at the top, underlined twice.
1101.ko: 첫마디부터 과감했던 당신이 밤새 딱 한 번 솔직해졌죠. '다리가 후들거려요.' 알렉스는 그 순간이 '귀엽다'고 했어요. 다음 주 월요일 계획표 맨 위엔 '특별 관리'라고 적혀 있어요. 밑줄도 두 번 그어져 있고요.

1110.name: 마무리만 얌전한 에이스
1110.nameEn: The Ace with a Gentle Ending
1110.en: Three bold answers in a row — then, at the cool-down, you suddenly chose the safest word in the room: 'safe.' Alex laughed for a long time. 'Even aces need a rest,' he said, and wrote something in his notebook that he would not show you.
1110.ko: 과감한 대답을 세 번 연속으로 하더니, 쿨다운에서 갑자기 제일 안전한 단어를 골랐죠 — '안전'. 알렉스는 한참을 웃었어요. '에이스도 쉬어야죠.' 그러고는 수첩에 뭔가 적었는데, 보여 주진 않더라고요.

1111.name: 겁 없는 풀코스
1111.en: Ready for anything, flexible, deal-making, and still asking for the best part at the end — Alex met his match tonight. He tears up next week's program and writes a new one titled 'Advanced.' The first line just says: 'Bring water.'
1111.ko: 뭐든 준비됐고, 유연했고, 협상까지 하고, 끝에서는 '제일 좋은 부분'까지 챙겼죠 — 알렉스는 오늘 밤 임자를 만났어요. 다음 주 프로그램을 찢어 버리고 '고급반'이라는 새 프로그램을 짜네요. 첫 줄엔 딱 한마디 — '물 챙겨 올 것.'
